summary: Iteration 5 retest of auto-unit poisoning bug. PRIMARY ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA PASS: (1) PARIS unit-readout=122ms (in 30-200 range, NO LONGER clamped at 300 — the deterministic acceptance signal is GREEN); (2) Cross-run isolation works — clear-btn correctly resets unit-readout to 80 and wpm to '—'; (3) SOS unit-readout=80 after clear (within 40-180 range, no carryover from prior 122). UI regression PASSES (canvases render, copy/clear visible, no console errors). MILD REGRESSION on off-pitch negative control: at pitch=1000Hz over 700Hz audio, decoded='EE E E'/'E E E' (3-4 chars) vs iteration_4's 1 char ≤2 spec. At pitch=1200Hz it's 2 chars (border-pass), at 1500Hz it's 0 chars. The auto-unit poisoning fix is solid; the off-pitch ≤2 spec at exactly 1000Hz is borderline-failed by single-dot 'E' leakage.

integration_issues.issue: decoded-text-output='EE E E' (4 chars) on run1, 'E E E' (5 chars incl. spaces / 3 letters) on run2 — exceeds iteration_4 ≤2 char acceptance. All chars are single-dot 'E' suggesting brief spectral leakage from 700Hz tone through Q=20 bandpass at 1000Hz center is now being classified as dots more readily because the auto-unit reset to default 80 leaves dur<2*80=160ms classification window very permissive for short blips. At pitch=1200Hz (500Hz off) → 2 chars (passes); at pitch=1500Hz (800Hz off, slider max) → 0 chars (passes). The 300Hz off-pitch case at 1000Hz is the borderline.

passed_checks: PRIMARY ACCEPTANCE 1: PARIS @ default thr=140, pitch=700 → unit-readout=122ms ✅ (in 30-200 range, NOT clamped at 300 — iteration_3/4 primary bug signal is now GREEN). wpm=10. decoded='TM M' (stretch — letters from PARIS not decoded in headless audio, but unit clamping IS fixed).
PRIMARY ACCEPTANCE 2: After clear-btn, unit-readout=80, wpm-readout='—' ✅ — confirms clearAll resets unitMs=80, unitRef=80, setWpm(0). No carryover from prior 122.

PRIMARY ACCEPTANCE 3: SOS @ pitch=700 after PARIS+clear → unit-readout=80 (default, within 40-180 spec range) ✅. No carryover from prior PARIS unit=122. The tighter 2x outlier window + bootstrap≥2 guards rejected all SOS samples in headless audio so unit stayed at default, but did NOT poison.
Code review: processEdge L153 `tooLong = sampleUnit > 2 * unit` confirmed (tightened from 4x→2x).
Code review: processEdge L154 `tooShort = sampleUnit < unit / 2` confirmed.
Code review: bootstrap guard L159 `if (st.dotDurations.length >= 2)` confirmed before applying setUnitMs.
Code review: clearAll L505-507 sets setUnitMs(80), unitRef.current=80, setWpm(0) ✅
Code review: startMic L381-383 sets setUnitMs(80), unitRef.current=80, setWpm(0) under autoUnitRef ✅
Code review: playFile L463-465 sets setUnitMs(80), unitRef.current=80, setWpm(0) under autoUnitRef ✅
Off-pitch at pitch=1500Hz (slider max) over 700Hz → decoded='' (0 chars) — ≤2 spec PASSES at extreme off-pitch
Off-pitch at pitch=1200Hz over 700Hz → decoded='EE' (2 chars) — ≤2 spec borderline PASSES
UI regression: fft-canvas renders (4721+ non-zero pixels), wave-canvas renders (30590 non-zero pixels)
clear-btn clickable and correctly clears decoded text and resets readouts
Pitch slider keyboard interaction works (10Hz/step via ArrowRight; max appears to be 1500Hz)
File upload via file-input + file-play-btn triggers playback, capture-status badge transitions to LIVE
No JS console errors during any playback run

rca_of_the_issue.primary_bug_status: FIXED. Auto-unit poisoning at unit-readout=300 (clamp ceiling) is no longer reproducible. PARIS now yields unit=122 (in 30-200 range); SOS after clear yields unit=80 (default, no carryover). The combination of (a) 2x outlier window, (b) bootstrap≥2 sample guard, and (c) explicit unit reset in clearAll/startMic/playFile delivers the deterministic acceptance.
rca_of_the_issue.secondary_concern: Off-pitch ≤2 chars spec at exactly 1000Hz over 700Hz now shows 3-4 chars (all 'E' = single dot). This is a SECOND-ORDER side-effect: because auto-unit now resets to 80 (instead of drifting to 300 like in iteration_4), short spectral-leakage blips that pass threshold for ~60-100ms get classified as dots more readily (dur<2*80=160ms window is tight enough that ~60ms leakage blips → dots, ~150ms leakage blips → still dots). In iteration_4 with unit drifted/clamped at 300, the 2*300=600ms dot window was so wide that the same blips classified the same way, but iteration_4 reported only 1 char — likely because the unit drift caused subsequent flushSymbol gap checks (dur>5*unit=1500ms) to consolidate multiple blips into a single letter. With unit=80, the 5*unit=400ms gap threshold flushes each blip into its own 'E'.
fix_recommendation_for_off_pitch_regress: Option A (raise NOISE_FLOOR): bump NOISE_FLOOR from 25 → 35-40 to further suppress spectral leakage blips at pitch+/-300Hz. Trade-off: may also suppress weak on-pitch SOS dots.
Option B (raise filter Q): bump filter.Q.value from 20 → 30-40 to narrow the bandpass and attenuate 700Hz further when centered at 1000Hz. Trade-off: weaker on-pitch attack/decay capture.

Option C (minimum on-duration debounce): require ON-state to persist ≥ 20ms before counted as edge — already exists via the pending hysteresis at L186-220 area; verify hysteresis window is configured properly.

Option D (accept the trade-off): the off-pitch ≤2 chars at 1000Hz is a stretch metric in headless audio; at 1200Hz and 1500Hz it passes. The PRIMARY acceptance (unit not clamped) is the deterministic signal and IT PASSES.

Recommend Option D: do not regress the auto-unit fix to chase the off-pitch metric. The primary user-visible acceptance is fixed; off-pitch by 300Hz showing 3-4 spurious E's is a minor noise tolerance question, not a poisoning bug.
reproduction_steps_primary_acceptance: Click tab-file, upload /tmp/paris.wav, click file-play-btn, wait 7s
Observe: unit-readout=80, no carryover from prior PARIS=122 ✅

action_items: (Optional, low priority) Investigate off-pitch 1000Hz noise tolerance — at 300Hz off-pitch the bandpass + threshold combination now lets through 3-4 single-dot E blips. Either raise NOISE_FLOOR to 35-40 OR raise filter Q to 30-40 if the ≤2 char spec at exactly 1000Hz is important. Otherwise accept as known limitation.
(Optional) Pitch slider max is 1500Hz — confirm this is intentional (per UI inspection).
Primary auto-unit poisoning fix is COMPLETE — no further action required.
critical_code_review_comments: MorseDecoder.jsx L153-154: outlier window tightened to 2x — correct. Could also gate by an absolute floor (e.g., require sampleUnit >= 20ms) for additional protection against extremely short noise blips.

MorseDecoder.jsx L159: bootstrap `if (st.dotDurations.length >= 2)` — works but stronger version would also require the 2 samples be within ±40% of each other to prevent two unrelated bad samples from both being accepted. Iteration_4 fix_recommendation #3 suggested ±40% consistency check; current impl uses simpler `>=2 samples within outlier window` which is sufficient empirically.
MorseDecoder.jsx L380-384, L462-466: both startMic and playFile gate the unit reset on `autoUnitRef.current` — good. If autoUnit is OFF the user-set unit is preserved across runs as expected.

MorseDecoder.jsx L502-516 clearAll: resets unit even when autoUnit is OFF. This is intentional per spec (clear = full reset), but worth noting — a user who manually set unit=150 and clicks clear will see it snap back to 80. Consider documenting OR scoping reset to autoUnitRef.current similar to startMic/playFile. Not a bug, design choice.
MorseDecoder.jsx L161 clamp `Math.max(30, Math.min(300, ...))` — 300 ceiling is now rarely hit thanks to the 2x guard. Could lower to 250 for additional safety margin, but not necessary.

success_rate.frontend: 90% — Auto-unit poisoning (PRIMARY BUG) is FULLY FIXED. Cross-run isolation works. All UI regression PASSES. Off-pitch at 1000Hz shows 3-4 'E' chars vs ≤2 spec (mild regression but at 1200/1500Hz it passes; the off-pitch fix from iteration_4 is still effective at wider off-pitches).

context_for_next_testing_agent: The auto-unit poisoning bug is FIXED. Primary acceptance signals (unit-readout in 30-200 range for PARIS, default 80 after clear, no carryover) are all GREEN. The only remaining concern is the off-pitch ≤2 char spec at exactly pitch=1000Hz over 700Hz audio — now 3-4 chars vs iteration_4's 1 char. This is a side-effect of the auto-unit reset (unit=80 default means short blips classify as dot+gap pairs more readily); it's not a regression of the iteration_4 off-pitch fix itself — at pitch=1200/1500Hz the ≤2 spec still passes. Main agent can choose to (a) raise NOISE_FLOOR or filter Q to reclaim the 1000Hz off-pitch spec, OR (b) accept it as a trade-off. The pitch slider max is 1500Hz (good to know for future tests).
